title: Podman with docker-compose does not set selinux labels
body:
### Issue Description

Podman with docker-compose generates the correct selinux labels for the directories, but they are not applied.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I created two directories and a `compose.yaml` file:

```bash
---
name: mariadbtest2
services:
  mariadb:
    image: docker.io/mariadb:latest
    container_name: mariadbtest2
    user: "50000:50000"
    userns_mode: "keep-id"
    volumes:
      # private
      - type: bind
        source: "mariadbtest2"
        target: "/var/lib/mysql"
        bind:
          create_host_path: false
          selinux: Z
    environment:
      - MARIADB_DATABASE=exmple-database
      - MARIADB_USER=example-user
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD=my_cool_secret
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=my-secret-pw
```

Start `mariadbtest1`:

```bash
podman run --detach --name mariadbtest1 --env MARIADB_USER=example-user --env MARIADB_PASSWORD=my_cool_secret --env MARIADB_DATABASE=exmple-database --env MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=my-secret-pw --userns keep-id --user "50000:50000" -v "./mariadbtest1:/var/lib/mysql:Z" docker.io/mariadb:latest
```

Start `mariadbtest2`:

```bash
podman compose up -d
```

### Describe the results you received

`ls -lZ` before the container starts:

```bash
ls -lZ
total 4
drwxr-xr-x. 1 podman podman unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0   0 Nov 24 16:21 mariadbtest1
drwxr-xr-x. 1 podman podman unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0   0 Nov 24 16:21 mariadbtest2
-rw-r--r--. 1 podman podman unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 510 Nov 24 16:31 compose.yaml
```

`ls -lZ` after the container starts:

```bash
ls -lZ
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 1 podman podman system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c152,c889 430 Nov 24 16:32 mariadbtest1
drwxr-xr-x. 1 podman podman unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0              0 Nov 24 16:21 mariadbtest2
-rw-r--r--. 1 podman podman unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0            520 Nov 24 16:33 compose.yaml
```

-> ❌ `mariadbtest2` has not been received the updated selinux labels.

Logs of `mariadbtest1`:

```bash
podman logs mariadbtest1
[...]
2025-11-24 16:32:17 0 [Note] Plugin 'wsrep-provider' is disabled.
2025-11-24 16:32:17 0 [Note] InnoDB: Buffer pool(s) load completed at 251124 16:32:17
2025-11-24 16:32:18 0 [Note] Server socket created on IP: '0.0.0.0', port: '3306'.
2025-11-24 16:32:18 0 [Note] Server socket created on IP: '::', port: '3306'.
2025-11-24 16:32:18 0 [Note] mariadbd: Event Scheduler: Loaded 0 events
2025-11-24 16:32:18 0 [Note] mariadbd: ready for connections.
Version: '12.1.2-MariaDB-ubu2404'  socket: '/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock'  port: 3306  mariadb.org binary distribution
```

-> ✅ works as expected

Logs of `mariadbtest2`:

```bash
podman logs mariadbtest2
2025-11-24 16:33:55+00:00 [Note] [Entrypoint]: Entrypoint script for MariaDB Server 1:12.1.2+maria~ubu2404 started.
2025-11-24 16:33:55+00:00 [Note] [Entrypoint]: Initializing database files
2025-11-24 16:33:56 0 [Warning] Can't create test file '/var/lib/mysql/c57c3ac155d3.lower-test' (Errcode: 13 "Permission denied")
2025-11-24 16:33:56 0 [ERROR] mariadbd: Can't create/write to file './ddl_recovery.log' (Errcode: 13 "Permission denied")
2025-11-24 16:33:56 0 [ERROR] DDL_LOG: Failed to create ddl log file: ./ddl_recovery.log
2025-11-24 16:33:56 0 [ERROR] Aborting
[...]
```

-> ❌ Does not work

-> Both containers have the correct selinux labels internally, but `mariadbtest1` uses `rbind` and `rprivate` while `mariadbtest2` uses `bind` (without r) and `private` (without r).

### Describe the results you expected

The directory `mariadbtest2` gets the label `system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c39,c838`

comments.body:
Are you using `podman-compose` or `docker-compose`? If you are using `podman-compose`, I suggest moving this issue to https://github.com/containers/podman-compose

(You can also use `docker-compose` with Podman, but the example you provided shows `podman compose`.)

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Honny1 I'm **not** using `podman-compose`, but `docker-compsoe` version `2.40.3` as can be seen in the `podman info output` section. What you have seen is the `podman compose` command that calls `docker-compose` in the background.

You may have overlook this, but in the `And if I diff both inspect files I can see these lines:` section I showed that the selinux labels have been **correctly created** as can be seen in the `podman inspect mariadbtest2` output, but they **have not been applied** to the system. So I don't see a `docker-compose` issue here as the containers will be started by Podman, so Podman has to apply the selinux labels?

I investigated the behaviour of Podman, Docker Compose (backed by the Podman service), and pure Docker Compose (backed by the Docker daemon). All Docker Compose configurations behave identically and do not change the directory label.

Assuming this was an issue on the Docker Compose side or with my `compose.yaml`, I created an alternative configuration:
```
---
name: mariadbtest5
services:
  mariadb:
    image: docker.io/mariadb:latest
    container_name: mariadbtest5
    user: "50000:50000"
    userns_mode: "keep-id"
    volumes:
      - ./mariadbtest5:/var/lib/mysql:Z
    environment:
      - MARIADB_DATABASE=exmple-database
      - MARIADB_USER=example-user
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD=my_cool_secret
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=my-secret-pw
```

This works as expected and correctly relabels the directory. I am closing this issue.
@Honny1 I created docker/compose#13396 to clarify the situation as `bind` mounts in short notation work, but the long notation does not.

@ndeloof explained in docker/compose#13396 that I have to use the short syntax to fix SELinux as the long syntax has a **different internal logic** initially meant to be used with Docker Swarm:

> Your 2nd example is equivalent to `create_host_path: false`. Using long syntax this attribute has no default value set
> You can use `docker compose config` to see the actual model used by compose after parsing
> 
> Basically, I would recommend not to use the long syntax but if you have to explicitly use an advanced feature. For a "classic" bind mount, always prefer the short syntax 

 _Originally posted by @ndeloof in [#13396](https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/13396#issuecomment-3581196083)_

> that should be documented
> 
> `volumes` long syntax was introduced to support docker swarm, and doesn't make any sense when used with a standalone docker engine. Actually, it would make more sense to introduce a `mount` section, that would reflect the `docker run  --mount` flag and its options. But we will have to support this "long syntax" anyway 

 _Originally posted by @ndeloof in [#13396](https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/13396#issuecomment-3581240275)_

@Honny1 So yes, you are right: The outcome of my config with the long notation syntax is the same with Podman and Docker, but the long notation syntax should not be used if the short one already suits the need as the long one is **(partly) incompatible with SELinux**  😀
basically: moby `mount` API doesn't offer an option to configure SELinux, this can't be used as a strict replacement for the legacy `bind`
